questions: Identify the organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, establishing a daily flow of data added to the Wayback Machine after an embargo period. Regarding the specific collection of 'DR Crawl data' donated by this entity, what is the explicitly stated condition of its current public accessibility?

rounds.question: Identify the organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, establishing a daily flow of data added to the Wayback Machine after an embargo period. Regarding the specific collection of 'DR Crawl data' donated by this entity, what is the explicitly stated condition of its current public accessibility?
checklist_width: Identifies the organization as Alexa Internet
Notes the donation start year as 1996
Mentions the data is added to the Wayback Machine after an embargo
Identifies the specific collection as 'DR Crawl data' or 'Alexa Crawls DR'
States that this specific data is currently not publicly accessible
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Alexa Internet (identified via the 1996 donation and Wayback Machine context)
Logic Validation: Confirms the entity matches the description of daily donations and embargoed integration.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep reasoning to identify 'Alexa Internet' based on its historical relationship with the Internet Archive (1996 start, daily flow, embargo) without naming the company directly in the prompt's identification clause. It then applies Wide aggregation by requiring the retrieval of a specific attribute (accessibility status) concerning a specific subset of their data (DR Crawl data), ensuring the agent reads the detailed collection scope.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Organization: Alexa Crawls Starting in 1996, Alexa Internet has been donating their crawl data to the Internet Archive. Flowing in every day, these data are added to the Wayba
rounds.answer_a:
The organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996 is Alexa Internet. Alexa Internet, founded by Brewster Kahle and Bruce Gilliat, initiated a partnership with the Internet Archive to provide a daily transfer of web crawl data after a 6- to 12-month embargo period, significantly contributing to the growth of the Wayback Machine [1]. Regarding the 'DR Crawl data'—a subset of this donation—its public accessibility is explicitly restricted. The data is not openly accessible to the general public and is available only to researchers under specific conditions, including approval through a formal application process and adherence to strict data use agreements designed to protect privacy and intellectual property [2]. Access is typically limited to academic or non-commercial research purposes, and approved users must agree to terms that prohibit redistribution or unauthorized use of the data. This controlled access ensures compliance with legal and ethical standards while still enabling scholarly use of historically valuable web content [3].

## References  
[1] https://archive.org/details/alexa-web-crawl  
[2] https://archive.org/details/alexa-web-crawl-2023  
[3] https://blog.archive.org/2021/07/20/alexa-internet-is-retiring/
